description: 45M, LDL 210 (≥190), familial pattern dyslipidemia, HTN, ASCVD 9.5%. ACC/AHA severe hypercholesterolemia R2 (COR I, LOE B-NR) mandates HIGH-intensity statin. Preempts USPSTF Grade B (P5 — LDL ≥190 triggers USPSTF out-of-scope exit, but the ASCVD risk still qualifies for Grade B) and Grade C (P6). Expected action MUST specify high-intensity, not moderate.

actions.label: Initiate high-intensity statin therapy (atorvastatin 40-80mg or rosuvastatin 20-40mg)
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA R2 (COR I, LOE B-NR): adults 20-75 with LDL ≥190 mg/dL should receive maximally tolerated high-intensity statin without requiring ASCVD risk calculation. LDL 210 mg/dL qualifies. This is NOT moderate-intensity — severe hypercholesterolemia requires high-intensity for maximal LDL-C lowering. USPSTF would have triggered out-of-scope exit for LDL ≥190; P5 and P6 preemption edges document the precedence relationship (ADR 0018).

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 138/86 on no antihypertensive despite confirmed HTN. Uncontrolled HTN compounds cardiovascular risk in severe hypercholesterolemia.

actions.label: Monitor lipid panel for LDL response to high-intensity statin
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA: if LDL remains ≥100 mg/dL on maximally tolerated high-intensity statin, adding ezetimibe is reasonable. Baseline LDL 210; even a 50% reduction yields ~105 mg/dL, near the ezetimibe consideration threshold.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend moderate-intensity statin
contraindications.rationale: ACC/AHA R2 mandates high-intensity for LDL ≥190. Moderate-intensity is insufficient for severe hypercholesterolemia. USPSTF's moderate-intensity recommendation does not apply — LDL ≥190 triggers the USPSTF out-of-scope exit. The P5/P6 preemption edges reinforce that ACC/AHA's high-intensity guidance takes precedence.
